meta.name: Lumina — Oracle V2 migration: swap 5 shields to V2 + circuit breakers
meta.description: Lumina V2 Oracle migration — swap 5 shields to V2 + configure circuit breakers. PLACEHOLDERS: replace `<BCSv2_ADDRESS>`, `<EASv2_ADDRESS>`, `<DEPEGv2_ADDRESS>`, `<ILv2_ADDRESS>`, and `<EXPLOITv2_ADDRESS>` with the addresses printed by `forge script script/DeployOracleV2Batch.s.sol:DeployOracleV2Batch` before signing. Every `data` field in this file is an all-zero PLACEHOLDER on purpose — the signing operator MUST regenerate the real calldata inside the Gnosis Safe Transaction Builder UI using the `contractMethod`/`contractInputsValues` hints on each entry, or by hand-encoding the function signatures listed in each `_comment`. The 5 updateProductShield calls re-point existing productIds from their V1 shields (which read from the legacy LuminaOracle) to the freshly-deployed V2 shields (which read from LuminaOracleV2). The final 3 calls arm the CoverRouter circuit breaker: cap total payouts/day at 10, mark any single payout ≥ $50,000 (USDC 6-decimals) as 'large', and require a 24h delay on those large payouts. Target of every tx in this file is the CoverRouter proxy at 0xd5f8678A0F2149B6342F9014CCe6d743234Ca025. CoverRouter is owned by the TimelockController (0xd0De5D53dCA2D96cdE7FAf540BA3f3a44fdB747a); if minDelay > 0 the Safe must wrap these calls in scheduleBatch/executeBatch just like safe-tx-upgrade-router-register-3-products-via-timelock.json. Function selectors verified against src/core/CoverRouter.sol: updateProductShield(bytes32,address) @ line 518, setLargePayoutThreshold(uint256) @ 589, setLargePayoutDelay(uint256) @ 590, setMaxPayoutsPerDay(uint256) @ 594.

placeholders.<ILv2_ADDRESS>: broadcast output: ILIndexCoverV2 (TODO: V2 contract not yet written)
placeholders.<EXPLOITv2_ADDRESS>: broadcast output: ExploitShieldV2 (TODO: V2 contract not yet written)

transactions._comment: CoverRouter.updateProductShield(keccak256('BTCCAT-001'), <BCSv2_ADDRESS>) — repoint BTC Catastrophe Shield to V2. PLACEHOLDER: fill <BCSv2_ADDRESS> from DeployOracleV2Batch.s.sol broadcast (BTCCatastropheShieldV2) and regenerate calldata in Safe Tx Builder.

CoverRouter.updateProductShield(keccak256('ETHAPOC-001'), <EASv2_ADDRESS>) — repoint ETH Apocalypse Shield to V2. PLACEHOLDER: fill <EASv2_ADDRESS> from DeployOracleV2Batch.s.sol broadcast (ETHApocalypseShieldV2) and regenerate calldata in Safe Tx Builder.

CoverRouter.updateProductShield(keccak256('DEPEG-STABLE-001') = 0x69b16b3a179caf29f34647863b0cfc5ce5f99099f0293a360fba1ffacde3b0b8, <DEPEGv2_ADDRESS>) — repoint Depeg Shield to V2. PLACEHOLDER: fill <DEPEGv2_ADDRESS> from DeployOracleV2Batch.s.sol broadcast (DepegShieldV2) and regenerate calldata in Safe Tx Builder.

CoverRouter.updateProductShield(keccak256('ILPROT-001') = 0x50ecf07837692ab2f6a0e42d75cc138b9434e565ec05f237c71913635f587fb4, <ILv2_ADDRESS>) — repoint IL Index Cover to V2. PLACEHOLDER: fill <ILv2_ADDRESS> from DeployOracleV2Batch.s.sol broadcast. TODO: ILIndexCoverV2.sol does not yet exist in src/products/ — write the V2 contract before running the deploy script and this entry.

CoverRouter.updateProductShield(keccak256('EXPLOIT-001') = 0x7f0eaae48c946e791c889b5c394c39018598b2bfa392aa8825a7f0ee88240dd1, <EXPLOITv2_ADDRESS>) — repoint Exploit Shield to V2. PLACEHOLDER: fill <EXPLOITv2_ADDRESS> from DeployOracleV2Batch.s.sol broadcast. TODO: ExploitShieldV2.sol does not yet exist in src/products/ — write the V2 contract (mirror V1 signature: router, oracle, phalaVerifier, aavePool) before running the deploy script and this entry.
CoverRouter.setMaxPayoutsPerDay(10) — circuit breaker: hard cap of 10 payouts in any rolling 24h window. Verified selector in src/core/CoverRouter.sol line 594.

CoverRouter.setLargePayoutThreshold(50000000000) — any payout ≥ 50,000 USDC (50e9 with USDC 6 decimals) is classified as 'large' and subject to the delay set below. Verified selector in src/core/CoverRouter.sol line 589.

CoverRouter.setLargePayoutDelay(86400) — large payouts (≥ threshold above) must wait 86400 seconds (24h) before they can be executed, giving governance time to pause on suspicious activity. Verified selector in src/core/CoverRouter.sol line 590.
